items.normalized_title: zero copy access to onelake data in azure databricks
items.lifecycle_group_key: zero copy access to onelake data in azure databricks
items.summary: OneLake catalog federation in Azure Databricks (Preview) enables Unity Catalog to query Fabric data stored in OneLake without copying, keeping OneLake as the source of truth while allowing Databricks compute to analyze tables through synchronized metadata and zero-copy data access. For more information, see Enable OneLake catalog federation .

items.unique_key: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/fundamentals/whats-new-archive|March 2026|GA|workspace level workload assignment and centralized admin
items.title: Workspace-level workload assignment and centralized admin
items.normalized_title: workspace level workload assignment and centralized admin
items.lifecycle_group_key: workspace level workload assignment and centralized admin
items.summary: Workspace admins can now add workloads directly to one or more workspaces. In the Workloads Hub , admins can add workloads directly to a workspace . The Fabric Admin Portal also now includes a dedicated Manage Workloads tab, a single pane of glass for workload governance across your organization.

items.title: Workspace-level workload assignment (Preview)
items.normalized_title: workspace level workload assignment
items.lifecycle_group_key: workspace level workload assignment
items.summary: Workspace admins can now add additional workloads directly to their workspaces , eliminating the need for tenant or capacity-level setup. In the Workloads Hub , admins can add workloads directly to a workspace .

items.title: Workspace-level surge protection (Preview)
items.normalized_title: workspace level surge protection
items.lifecycle_group_key: workspace level surge protection
items.summary: Workspace-level surge protection controls (Preview) enable per-workspace CU percentage limits over a rolling 24-hour period, automatic blocking of workspaces exceeding thresholds, and mission critical mode to exempt high-priority workspaces from surge protection rules. For more information, see Surge protection .

items.title: Workspace-level surge protection (Preview)

items.title: Workspace-level Private Link (Generally Available)
items.normalized_title: workspace level private link
items.lifecycle_group_key: workspace level private link
items.summary: Fabric workspace-level Private Link , now generally available, enables fine-grained network isolation by securing individual Fabric workspaces with private endpoints. For more information, see Private link for Fabric workspaces .

items.title: Workspace tags (Generally Available)
items.normalized_title: workspace tags
items.lifecycle_group_key: workspace tags
items.summary: Workspace tags add shared context at the workspace level, so teams can organize workspaces by team, project, or cost center, filter workspaces by tags in the workspaces list and OneLake Catalog Explorer, and manage tags at scale by using APIs. For more information, see Fabric tags overview and Define tags .

For more information, see Workspace outbound access protection .

items.title: Workspace monitoring (Preview)
items.normalized_title: workspace monitoring
items.lifecycle_group_key: workspace monitoring
items.summary: Workspace monitoring (preview) is a Microsoft Fabric database that collects data from a range of Fabric items in your workspace, and lets you access and analyze logs and metrics. For more information about this feature, see Announcing preview of workspace monitoring .

items.title: Workspace customer-managed keys for BYOK (Preview)
items.normalized_title: workspace customer managed keys for byok
items.lifecycle_group_key: workspace customer managed keys for byok
items.summary: Workspace CMK for BYOK (Preview) enables workspace-level customer-managed key encryption in BYOK-enabled Fabric capacities, so you can use the same or separate Azure Key Vault keys without dedicated capacities. For more information, see Customer-managed keys for Fabric workspaces .

items.title: Workspace customer-managed keys for BYOK (Preview)
items.normalized_title: workspace customer managed keys for byok
items.lifecycle_group_key: workspace customer managed keys for byok
items.summary: Workspace customer-managed keys (CMK) for BYOK (Preview) enables workspace-level CMK in BYOK-enabled Fabric capacities, so you can use the same or separate Azure Key Vault keys for Power BI semantic models and other Fabric items without provisioning dedicated capacities. For more information, see Customer-managed keys for Fabric workspaces .

items.title: Workspace Outbound Access Protection for Spark (Preview)
items.normalized_title: workspace outbound access protection for spark
items.lifecycle_group_key: workspace outbound access protection for spark
items.summary: Workspace Outbound Access Protection for Spark lets workspace admins restrict Spark outbound connections to only approved destinations via managed private endpoints to reduce data exfiltration risk. For more information, see Workspace outbound access protection (preview) .

items.normalized_title: workspace outbound access protection for data factory and onelake shortcuts
items.lifecycle_group_key: workspace outbound access protection for data factory and onelake shortcuts
items.summary: Workspace Outbound Access Protection (Preview) extends to Pipelines, Copy job, Dataflows, OneLake Shortcuts, and Mirrored databases. Workspace admins can configure allow and deny rules for outbound connections to external data sources and gateways, which prevents data exfiltration. For more information, see Workspace outbound access protection (Generally Available) and Managing outbound access from OneLake with outbound access protection .

items.title: Workspace Monitoring for Copy job (Preview)
items.normalized_title: workspace monitoring for copy job
items.lifecycle_group_key: workspace monitoring for copy job
items.summary: Workspace Monitoring for Copy job (Preview) streams Copy job execution logs to a Monitoring Eventhouse with per-activity metrics including throughput, data volume, error codes, and timing for centralized observability. For more information, see Workspace monitoring for Copy job .

For more information, see Workspace Monitoring for Copy job .

items.title: Workspace IP firewall rules (Generally Available)
items.normalized_title: workspace ip firewall rules
items.lifecycle_group_key: workspace ip firewall rules
items.summary: Workspace IP firewall rules let workspace admins define an allow list of public IP addresses or IP ranges that can access a Fabric workspace, helping limit inbound access to approved network locations. For more information, see Protect workspaces by using IP firewall rules .

items.title: Warehouse source control (Preview)
items.normalized_title: warehouse source control
items.lifecycle_group_key: warehouse source control
items.summary: Using Source control (preview) , you can manage development and deployment of versioned warehouse objects. You can use SQL Database Projects extension available inside of Visual Studio Code . For more information on warehouse source control, see CI/CD with Warehouses in Microsoft Fabric .

items.title: Warehouse snapshots (preview)
items.normalized_title: warehouse snapshots
items.lifecycle_group_key: warehouse snapshots
items.summary: Warehouse snapshots are a point-in-time, read-only representation of your data warehouse. You can create a snapshot of your warehouse at any point in the past 30 days, connect to it and query it just like a warehouse, and "roll forward" your snapshot regularly. To get started, see Create and manage a warehouse snapshot .

items.unique_key: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/fundamentals/whats-new-archive|January 2025|GA|warehouse restore points and restore in place
items.title: Warehouse restore points and restore in place
items.normalized_title: warehouse restore points and restore in place
items.lifecycle_group_key: warehouse restore points and restore in place
items.summary: You can now create restore points and perform an in-place restore of a warehouse to a past point in time. Restore in-place is an essential part of data warehouse recovery , which allows to restore the data warehouse to a prior known reliable state by replacing or over-writing the existing data warehouse from which the restore point was created.

items.title: Warehouse data clustering (Preview)
items.normalized_title: warehouse data clustering
items.lifecycle_group_key: warehouse data clustering
items.summary: Data clustering is a technique used to organize and store data based on similarity. Data clustering improves query performance and reduces compute and storage access costs for queries by grouping similar records together. For more information and to get started, see Data clustering in Fabric Data Warehouse and Use data clustering in Fabric Data Warehouse (Preview) .

items.title: Warehouse custom SQL pools (preview)
items.normalized_title: warehouse custom sql pools
items.lifecycle_group_key: warehouse custom sql pools
items.summary: Custom SQL pools (Preview) give workspace administrators fine-grained control over SQL compute resource allocation, enabling you to define pools, assign percentage-based compute, and route queries by application name. For more information, see Custom SQL pools .

items.title: Warehouse connection string changes (Generally Available)
items.normalized_title: warehouse connection string changes
items.lifecycle_group_key: warehouse connection string changes
items.summary: Connection string behavior changes in Fabric Data Warehouse make connections deterministic when the Initial Catalog value is ambiguous or missing, improving predictability for downstream auditing. For more information, see Fabric Data Warehouse connectivity and Operation list .

items.title: Warehouse SQL Audit Logs (Generally Available)
items.normalized_title: warehouse sql audit logs
items.lifecycle_group_key: warehouse sql audit logs
items.summary: SQL Audit Logs in Fabric Data Warehouse and SQL analytics endpoints provide a comprehensive and immutable record of all database activities. They capture critical details such as the event timestamp, the user or process that triggered the action, and the executed T-SQL statements. To get started, see How to configure SQL audit logs .

items.title: Warehouse IDENTITY columns (Preview)
items.normalized_title: warehouse identity columns
items.lifecycle_group_key: warehouse identity columns
items.summary: IDENTITY columns automatically produce unique values for each new row, eliminating the need for manual key assignments and eliminating the risk of key duplication and key integrity issues. For more information and to get started, see IDENTITY columns (Preview) and Use IDENTITY columns to create surrogate keys . You can also learn how to Migrate to IDENTITY columns in Fabric Data Warehouse .

items.normalized_title: visual sql audit logs configuration
items.lifecycle_group_key: visual sql audit logs configuration
items.summary: A new visual experience for configuring, enabling, and managing SQL Audit Logs in Fabric Warehouse is now available as a preview feature , allowing you to set up and customize auditing directly from the Fabric portal. For more information, see SQL audit logs in Fabric Data Warehouse (Preview) . To get started, see Configure SQL audit logs in Fabric Data Warehouse .

items.summary: The Variable library allows you to define and manage variables at the workspace level, so you can use them across various workspace items, such as pipelines, notebooks, Shortcut for lakehouse, and more. You can also Use variable libraries in CI/CD for Dataflow Gen 2 and Copy Job .

items.normalized_title: variable libraries
items.lifecycle_group_key: variable libraries
items.summary: The Variable library is a new Fabric item that functions as a bucket of variables that can be consumed by other items in the workspace, and is integrated with notebooks. For more information, see Variable library (preview) and Notebook integration with variable libraries .

items.title: Variable Library Support in Notebook (Generally Available)
items.normalized_title: variable library support in notebook
items.lifecycle_group_key: variable library support in notebook
items.summary: Use variable libraries in notebooks to centralize configuration . You can access variables with notebookutils.variableLibrary , inject environment-specific settings via %%configure , and enable secure CI/CD with Service Principal support. For more information, see Get started with variable libraries .

items.normalized_title: teams and outlook activities in pipelines
items.lifecycle_group_key: teams and outlook activities in pipelines
items.summary: The activities that allow you to send email or Teams messages from Fabric pipelines are now generally available. For more information, see the Outlook 356 activity and the Teams activity .

items.normalized_title: task flows in microsoft fabric
items.lifecycle_group_key: task flows in microsoft fabric
items.summary: With Task flows , now generally available, you no longer need to use a whiteboard to sketch out the different parts of the project and their interrelationships. Instead, you can use a task flow to build and bring this key information into the project itself. For more information, see Task flows in Microsoft Fabric (Generally Available) .

items.title: Tags
items.normalized_title: tags
items.lifecycle_group_key: tags
items.summary: Tags help admins categorize and organize data , enhancing the searchability of your data and boosting success rates and efficiency for end users. Tags in Microsoft Fabric are now generally available.

items.title: Tabbed multitasking and object explorer (Generally Available)
items.normalized_title: tabbed multitasking and object explorer
items.lifecycle_group_key: tabbed multitasking and object explorer
items.summary: Tabbed multitasking and object explorer (Generally Available) deliver fully supported tabs for working across multiple Fabric items in parallel, plus an object explorer that browses items across open workspaces. The GA release adds right-click tab management, open-in-new-window, a resizable explorer pane, support for opening monitoring jobs as tabs, and accessibility improvements. For more information, see Multitask with tabs and object explorer .

items.title: T-SQL notebooks GA
items.normalized_title: t sql notebooks
items.lifecycle_group_key: t sql notebooks

The new monitoring experience includes a new Recent run panel and a dedicated T-SQL panel to list the query history. To learn more, see T-SQL support in Microsoft Fabric notebooks .

You can enable event log compression and rolling for Spark Streaming jobs. For more information, see Use extended Apache Spark history server to debug and diagnose Apache Spark applications .

items.lifecycle_group_key: fabric mcp
items.summary: Fabric MCP is a developer-focused Model Context Protocol server that enables AI-assisted code generation and item authoring in Microsoft Fabric. Designed for agent-powered development and automation, it integrates with tools like VS Code and GitHub Codespaces as part of the Microsoft MCP initiative. For more information, see Introducing Fabric MCP (preview) .

items.unique_key: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/fabric/fundamentals/whats-new-archive|April 2026|Preview|fabric local mcp and fabric remote mcp
items.title: Fabric Local MCP (Generally Available) and Fabric Remote MCP (Preview)
items.normalized_title: fabric local mcp and fabric remote mcp
items.lifecycle_group_key: fabric local mcp and fabric remote mcp
items.summary: Agentic Fabric: How MCP is turning your data platform into an AI-native operating system introduces two Model Context Protocol entry points to Fabric: Local MCP, an open-source on-machine server that grounds AI assistants in Fabric APIs, OneLake file operations, and Fabric item operations, and Remote MCP, a cloud-hosted server that lets agents perform authenticated workspace, item, search, permission, and connection operations through Microsoft Entra ID with full audit logging. The Fabric Remote MCP server is also available as a preview feature. For more information, see Get started with Fabric Pro-Dev MCP Server and Get started with Fabric Core MCP Server .

items.title: Fabric Local MCP (Generally Available)
items.normalized_title: fabric local mcp
items.lifecycle_group_key: fabric local mcp

The Fabric Remote MCP server is also available as a preview feature. For more information, see Get started with Fabric Pro-Dev MCP Server .

We also created a new Fabric Community Repository . This repository contains a wide variety of item types built with the Extensibility toolkit you can add to your tenant. For more information, see Introducing the Microsoft Fabric Extensibility Toolkit .

items.title: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit (Generally Available)
items.normalized_title: fabric extensibility toolkit
items.lifecycle_group_key: fabric extensibility toolkit
items.summary: The Extensibility Toolkit creates the opportunity for partners and customers to build, validate, and publish custom Fabric workloads to production with full Microsoft support. The Fabric Community Repository contains a wide variety of item types built with the Extensibility toolkit that you can add to your tenant. CI/CD, Remote Lifecycle Notifications, and Fabric Scheduler (Preview) have also recently been added to the Extensibility Toolkit. For more information, see Extensibility Toolkit documentation .

items.lifecycle_group_key: fabric eventstream derived streams in direct ingestion mode
items.summary: You can ingest data from Fabric Eventstream to Eventhouse seamlessly either from an eventstream or using Eventhouse Get Data Wizard. This capability is now being extended to support Eventstream Derived streams in direct ingestion mode . For more information, see Fabric Eventhouse now supports Eventstream Derived Streams in Direct Ingestion mode (preview) .

items.title: Fabric Data Warehouse proactive statistics refresh
items.normalized_title: fabric data warehouse proactive statistics refresh
items.lifecycle_group_key: fabric data warehouse proactive statistics refresh
items.summary: With this feature enabled, column statistics that are automatically created during SELECT queries are now eligible to be proactively refreshed after data changes. This reduces the chances of a user query having to wait for statistic updates before being able to run, reducing user query time. For more information, see Fabric Data Warehouse Statistics .

items.title: Fabric Data Warehouse incremental statistics refresh
items.normalized_title: fabric data warehouse incremental statistics refresh
items.lifecycle_group_key: fabric data warehouse incremental statistics refresh
items.summary: Incremental statistics refresh is a performance optimization to automatic statistic updates. It opportunistically reduces the duration of automatic statistic updates by only sampling newly added rows (instead of the entire column), speeding up user queries that must update statistics before running. For more information, see Fabric Data Warehouse Statistics .

items.normalized_title: deltaflow transformation in fabric real time intelligence s eventstream
items.lifecycle_group_key: deltaflow transformation in fabric real time intelligence s eventstream
items.summary: DeltaFlow is a capability in Fabric Eventstream that transforms raw Change Data Capture (CDC) events into a flattened, analytics-ready format. Instead of working with deeply nested Debezium JSON payloads, DeltaFlow produces tabular rows that closely mirror the structure of the source database tables, enriched with metadata columns that describe each change. Eventstream supports DeltaFlow transformations, which are currently in preview. For more information, see DeltaFlow output transformation (preview) and Blog: Business Events in Microsoft Fabric (Preview) .

The Livy API offers the ability to customize the execution environment through its integration with the Environment .

items.title: Multimodal support in Fabric AI functions (Preview)
items.normalized_title: multimodal support in fabric ai functions
items.lifecycle_group_key: multimodal support in fabric ai functions
items.summary: AI functions can process images (JPG/JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP), PDFs, and common text formats alongside text data. Most functions accept file-path inputs via column_type="path" . New helpers include aifunc.load (folder-to-table ingestion with optional prompt and schema), aifunc.list_file_paths (file URL enumeration), and ai.infer_schema (schema inference compatible with ai.extract ). For more information, see Transform and enrich data with AI functions and Extract information (ai.extract) .

items.title: Activator rules for OneLake items (Preview)
items.normalized_title: activator rules for onelake items
items.lifecycle_group_key: activator rules for onelake items
items.summary: In the Fabric portal, you can create and manage Activator rules for OneLake items using Fabric Activator. These rules allow you to automate actions based on specific events or conditions related to OneLake items, such as when a file is created or deleted. You can also create rules based on the status of processes that interact with OneLake items, such as pipelines, Spark jobs, and notebooks. For more information, see Trigger Fabric items .

items.lifecycle_group_key: custom ca and mtls support in eventstream connectors
items.summary: Custom CA and mTLS support in Eventstream connectors (Preview) lets you specify custom certificate authority and client certificates stored in your own Azure Key Vault when configuring a source, so Eventstream connectors can connect to Kafka-based sources (Apache Kafka, AWS MSK, Confluent Cloud for Apache Kafka) and Confluent Schema Registry that aren't covered by the predefined trusted CA list or that require mTLS. For more information, see Eventstream sources overview .
